pro-features.meta.title: Copilot Pro Features - DevAI Day Workshop
pro-features.meta.description: Explore advanced GitHub Copilot features for professional development. Learn about Coding Agent, custom agents, PR generation, and code review.
pro-features.sidebar.title: Copilot Pro Features
pro-features.progress.label: Progress
pro-features.step1.title: Create New Repo with Coding Agent
pro-features.step1.p1: The Copilot Coding Agent can create entire repositories from scratch based on your requirements. This is ideal for bootstrapping new projects quickly.
pro-features.step1.p2: Follow these steps to create a new repository using the Copilot Coding Agent:
pro-features.step1.li1: Log in to github.com with your GitHub account
pro-features.step1.li2: Click the + icon in the top right and select New repository
pro-features.step1.li4: Add a Description for your project (e.g., "An enterprise employee management system with CRUD operations")
pro-features.step1.li5: In the text box at the bottom, add your app requirements and click Create repository
pro-features.step1.fig1: Create a new repository with Copilot Coding Agent
pro-features.step1.p3: Here's an example of what you can enter in the requirement textbox:
pro-features.step1.p4: You can also use Copilot to generate ideas and create issues for existing applications. Here's an example prompt to create enhancement issues:
pro-features.step1.fig2: Copilot generates structured issues based on your requirements
pro-features.step1.li6: Review and edit each issue as needed
pro-features.step1.li8: In the Assignees section, select Copilot to assign the Coding Agent
pro-features.step1.fig3: Assign Copilot as the implementer for your issue
pro-features.step1.p6: The Coding Agent works asynchronously. It will create a branch, implement the changes, and open a Pull Request automatically.
pro-features.step1.image1.alt: Creating a new repository with Copilot Coding Agent
pro-features.step1.image3.alt: Assigning Coding Agent to an issue
pro-features.step2.title: Plan & Design Document Generation
pro-features.step2.p1: Use Custom Agents to generate comprehensive planning and design documents for your projects.
pro-features.step2.p2: Custom Agents are specialized AI assistants you can create for specific tasks. They can be configured with specific instructions, capabilities, and constraints.
pro-features.step2.p4: Refer the Custom Agents from Awesome Copilot Repository.
pro-features.step2.p5: Once created, you can invoke the custom agent in Copilot Agent sessions. The agent will generate design documents following your specified format.
pro-features.step2.fig1: Select Custom Agent as the implementer for your task
pro-features.step2.image1.alt: Custom Agent generating a design document
pro-features.step3.title: Develop Functionality with Coding Agent
pro-features.step3.p1: The Copilot Coding Agent can implement entire features autonomously, including running tests and fixing issues.
pro-features.step3.p2: When you assign the Coding Agent to an issue:
pro-features.step3.li1: Analysis: The agent reads the issue, related code, and project structure
pro-features.step3.li2: Planning: It creates a plan for the implementation
pro-features.step3.li3: Implementation: Writes code, creates files, and modifies existing ones
pro-features.step3.li4: Testing: Runs tests and fixes any failures
pro-features.step3.li5: Pull Request: Opens a PR with a detailed description
pro-features.step3.fig1: Track the Coding Agent's progress in the Pull Requests page
pro-features.step3.p3: After a few minutes, you'll see:
pro-features.step3.li7: Pull Request: With detailed description of changes
pro-features.step3.fig2: A complete Pull Request generated by the Coding Agent
pro-features.step3.p4: The Coding Agent uses GitHub MCP Server and Playwright MCP Server by default, enabling it to visually verify implementations and interact with GitHub APIs.
pro-features.step4.title: Copilot Instructions
pro-features.step4.p1: Copilot Instructions help you customize how Copilot generates code and reviews your work. They're essential for maintaining consistency across your team.
pro-features.step4.p2: Create a file at .github/copilot-instructions.md in your repository:
pro-features.step4.li1: Consistency: All Copilot suggestions follow your team's conventions
pro-features.step4.li2: Onboarding: New team members learn patterns through AI suggestions
pro-features.step4.li3: Quality: Reduces review cycles by getting things right the first time
pro-features.step4.li4: Documentation: Serves as living documentation of coding standards
pro-features.step4.p3: Check out Awesome Copilot Repository for great examples of custom instructions you can adapt for your project.
pro-features.step5.title: Generate PR & Code Review
pro-features.step5.p1: Copilot can help you generate Pull Request descriptions and review code. This is especially useful for solo developers or small teams.
pro-features.step5.p2: When creating a Pull Request, use Copilot to generate a comprehensive description:
pro-features.step5.li1: Open the Pull Request creation page
pro-features.step5.fig1: Click the Copilot icon to generate a PR summary
pro-features.step5.p3: Copilot generates a summary based on your commits:
pro-features.step5.fig2: Generated PR description with context and changes
pro-features.step5.p4: Add Copilot as a reviewer to get AI-powered code review:
pro-features.step5.li4: In the Reviewers section, click to add a reviewer
pro-features.step5.fig3: Add Copilot as a PR reviewer
pro-features.step5.li6: Pull Request Overview: Summary of code changes
pro-features.step5.p6: Enable automatic Copilot reviews via Settings → Branches → Rulesets → "Require a pull request before merging" → "Automatically request Copilot code review"
pro-features.step6.title: Copilot Spaces
pro-features.step6.p1: Copilot Spaces help you organize project knowledge and provide context to Copilot for better code suggestions and understanding.
pro-features.step6.p2: Copilot Spaces allow you to create a shared context for your project by attaching relevant documents, files, and resources that Copilot can reference when generating code or answering questions.
pro-features.step6.li3: Give your Space a descriptive name (e.g., "Employee Management App")
pro-features.step6.fig1: Create a new Copilot Space for your project
pro-features.step6.p3: Add relevant files and documents to help Copilot understand your project:
pro-features.step6.li7: API specifications, design documents, and technical specs
pro-features.step6.fig2: Attach project documentation to your Space
pro-features.step6.p4: Once your Space is set up, you can:
pro-features.step6.li8: Ask questions: Query Copilot about your project architecture or codebase
pro-features.step6.li9: Get context-aware suggestions: Copilot references your attached files for better code completion
pro-features.step6.li10: Share with team: Collaborate with team members using the same context
pro-features.step6.p5: With your architecture document attached, you can ask:
pro-features.step6.p6: Keep your Space updated as your project evolves. Regularly add new documentation and remove outdated files to ensure Copilot has the most current context.
pro-features.step7.title: Agent Mission Control
pro-features.step7.p1: Agent Mission Control gives you visibility and control over all Coding Agent activities in your organization.
pro-features.step7.p3: Track what the Coding Agent is doing across your repositories:
pro-features.step7.li1: Active Tasks: See which issues the agent is currently working on
pro-features.step7.li3: Error States: Identify tasks that need intervention
pro-features.step7.p4: You can guide the agent's work in real-time:
pro-features.step7.p5: Add comments to PRs where agents are actively working, and request changes as needed:
pro-features.step7.p6: Ask the agent to do more in the PR comments:
pro-features.step7.li4: Clear Issues: Write detailed issue descriptions with acceptance criteria
pro-features.step7.li5: Small Scope: Break large features into smaller, focused issues
pro-features.step7.li6: Review Promptly: The sooner you review, the sooner you can iterate
pro-features.step7.li7: Provide Feedback: Help the agent learn your preferences through comments
pro-features.step7.p7: You've completed the "Copilot Pro Features" learning path! You've learned:
pro-features.step7.li8: Using the Coding Agent to implement features autonomously
pro-features.step7.li9: Creating custom agents and prompt files
pro-features.step7.li10: Setting up Copilot Instructions for consistent code
pro-features.step7.li11: Generating PR descriptions and conducting AI-powered reviews
pro-features.step7.li13: Apply these features to your real projects
pro-features.step7.li14: Create custom agents for your team's specific needs
pro-features.step7.li15: Explore GitHub Copilot Documentation for advanced features
